title: linaria viscosa subsp. salzmannii
intro: Linaria viscosa subsp. salzmannii, also known as the sticky toadflax, is a plant species within the toadflax genus, characterized by its sticky stems and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its alkaloid and saponin content. The health benefits associated with Linaria viscosa subsp. salzmannii include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential in lowering blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant may be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these preparations.
